results.title: Acoustic Insulation or Soundproofing Options for Older Flats via Home Improvement Programmes
results.summary: The Minister for National Development clarified that acoustic insulation or soundproofing is not within the scope of the Home Improvement Programme (HIP). HIP is strictly limited to addressing safety and common maintenance issues caused by wear and tear in older flats. Consequently, the Ministry will not consider incorporating noise mitigation measures into this specific programme. Households seeking soundproofing solutions must look outside the HIP framework, as no targeted measures for chronic external noise are currently planned under this initiative.

results.title: Adapting Structural Envelope of Existing North East Line Station Boxes to Accommodate Platform Elongation for Longer Train Sets
results.summary: The Acting Minister for Transport stated that extending the North East Line beyond its current six-car train capacity requires major modifications to underground stations, platforms, tunnels, and tracks, including turnback sidings and depot stabling. These works necessitate heavy excavation, which would halt services for at least a year, alongside significant land acquisition to extend stations. Consequently, the current structural envelope cannot accommodate longer train sets without these extensive disruptions. Instead, the government is prioritizing the upcoming Cross Island Line and studying the feasibility of the Seletar Line to enhance regional connectivity and capacity.

results.title: Addressing Cost Concerns of Singapore Taxi and Private Hire Drivers Given New Cross-border Arrangements for Ride-hailing Services
results.summary: The Acting Minister for Transport clarified that while operational cost equalization between Singaporean and Malaysian taxis is not feasible, the Cross-Border Taxi Scheme enhances viability by restricting foreign taxis to designated pick-up points in Malaysia, whereas local taxis may pick up passengers anywhere in their home country. The Land Transport Authority will enforce compliance with Singapore's ERP2 on-board unit requirements, specific livery standards, and tamper-proof plates for licensed cross-border vehicles. Additionally, plans are underway to increase ride-hail pick-up points at frequented locations to bolster demand for return trips to Singapore. Fuel subsidies remain a matter for Malaysian authorities to consider.

results.title: AED Installation in New Estates Such as Tengah
results.summary: Before Automated External Defibrillator (AED) installation in new estates, the Singapore Civil Defence Force (SCDF) must complete infrastructural and security works, including CCTV coverage to deter theft. Twenty-nine AEDs are currently installed in Tengah, with 11 more scheduled for deployment by February 2026 to cover approximately 70% of occupied HDB blocks; installation will continue throughout 2026. Pending full deployment, SCDF organises community emergency preparedness events like Community Resilience Days and MyNiceHome roadshows in partnership with the People's Association and HDB. Additionally, the "My Responder Plus" programme enables community collaboration with SCDF to organise activities equipping residents with life-saving skills, while Community Emergency and Engagement Committees are encouraged to partner with SCDF for further programmes.

results.title: Adequacy of Clementi Public Library's Space and Programme Capacity Given Upcoming BTOs, Planned Cross Island Line Interchange and Ageing Population
results.summary: Clementi Public Library recorded approximately 700,000 visits in 2025, a decrease from 800,000 in 2023, indicating it currently operates within capacity limits. The National Library Board (NLB) supports this facility with a 24-hour digital library and continuously reviews collections to meet evolving community needs. As part of these efforts, Clementi Public Library is scheduled for a revamp to enhance the patron experience, with further details to be announced when ready. The NLB has not confirmed plans for expansion or relocation at this time.

results.title: Adequacy of Construction Guidelines and Building Codes in Event of Seismic Actions and Plans to Enhance Adequacy
results.summary: Singapore is classified as a low-seismicity region, so seismic design requirements currently apply only to new buildings above 20 metres on soft soil. The Minister confirmed there are no plans to extend these regulations to buildings 20 metres or below. While existing codes ensure structures withstand lateral forces from distant tremors, the Building and Construction Authority will continue reviewing safety requirements against scientific evidence and international standards. No immediate revisions to include lower-rise buildings are scheduled.

results.title: Adjournment of Debate
results.summary: Mr Zaqy Mohamad moved to adjourn the debate on the motion. The Speaker was informed that the debate would resume the following day. No further policy measures, legislative changes, or operational constraints were discussed during this brief procedural exchange. The item serves solely to conclude the current session and schedule the continuation of the debate for tomorrow.

results.title: Administrative Requirements and Procedures Imposed by Insurers for Cancer Drug Claims
results.summary: Claims for MediSave, MediShield Life, and Integrated Shield Plans are filed electronically via the national claims processing system, which includes all necessary data fields for Cancer Drug List (CDL) assessments to minimize administrative burden. For non-CDL treatments covered by riders or cases involving providers unable to use the system, insurers may require manual submissions or additional information regarding regulatory approval and clinical guidelines to process reimbursements correctly. The Ministry of Health acknowledges these requirements and will engage insurers and healthcare providers to streamline processes if specific feedback highlights opportunities for improvement.

results.title: Aligning Silver Support Scheme's Monthly Income Threshold with Local Qualifying Salary Quantum
results.summary: The Minister clarified that the Silver Support Scheme's $1,500 monthly per capita household income threshold is distinct from the Local Qualifying Salary (LQS) of $1,600, as the former targets retirement adequacy while the latter ensures meaningful local wages to prevent foreign worker quota abuse. Consequently, the two metrics are not correlated, and the Ministry does not track statistics on seniors pushed into lower benefit tiers due to LQS adjustments. The scheme was recently enhanced in 2025 by raising the qualifying monthly per capita household income threshold from $1,800 to $2,300, a 28% increase intended to include more seniors with limited family support. The Ministry reaffirmed its commitment to periodic reviews of the Silver Support Scheme without linking it to future LQS refinements.

results.title: Approval Rate for LTVP Applications from Spouses of Singapore Citizens over Past Three Years
results.summary: From 2022 to 2024, the average annual approval rate for Long-Term Visit Pass (LTVP) applications from spouses of Singapore citizens was approximately 91%. This statistic reflects the approval performance for this specific category over the three-year period. The Coordinating Minister for National Security and Minister for Home Affairs provided this figure in response to a parliamentary question regarding the approval trends for LTVP holders who are spouses of citizens.

results.title: Arbitrary Parking Fine Enforcement by Regulated Carpark Management Companies and Safeguarding Motorists' Rights
results.summary: The Ministry of Transport and Land Transport Authority (LTA) received four complaints regarding arbitrary fines from regulated car park management companies over the past two years. Private parking spaces are managed by premise owners or Management Corporation Strata Title (MCST) entities, which must adhere to LTA's Code of Practice ensuring clear signage on charges and fines. To safeguard motorists' rights, LTA engages relevant owners or MCSTs to address issues, while motorists may also apply to the Strata Titles Board for mediation between involved parties.

results.title: Assessment of Singapore's Defence Ecosystem's Competitiveness and Growth in Defence Export Value
results.summary: The Ministry of Defence and Singapore Armed Forces regularly review operational needs to develop solutions ahead of emerging threats, incorporating global conflict lessons. While local companies have developed overseas products based on SAF experiences, the Ministry does not disclose specific capabilities, build-up plans, or commercial investment data unless useful for deterrence. Consequently, the Minister declined to provide the requested percentage increase in defence export value over five years or confirm specific expansions in local manufacturing and research capacity. Details on these commercial metrics remain undisclosed to avoid compromising operational security.

results.title: Avenues of Recourse for Consumers Facing Predatory Practices by Blind Box or Gacha Mechanism Service Providers
results.summary: Under the Consumer Protection (Fair Trading) Act 2003, blind box and 'gacha' mechanisms are permitted provided businesses do not make false claims about item odds. Consumers facing unfair practices may approach the Consumers Association of Singapore (CASE) for negotiation, mediation, or referral to the Competition and Consumer Commission of Singapore (CCS) for investigation. Both agencies regularly conduct outreach to educate the public on consumer rights and available redress avenues. The Minister clarified that while specific items are not guaranteed, misleading representations regarding probabilities constitute an unfair practice subject to regulatory action.

results.title: Breakdown of Property Ownership Value across Income Groups by Singaporean and Non-Singaporean Households in Last 10 Years
results.summary: The Minister for National Development provided median values of owner-occupied residential property for resident households, broken down by income deciles, based on the Household Expenditure Survey. These figures cover the last 10 years. The Government explicitly stated it does not collect data on the value of residential property owned by non-resident households, meaning no breakdown for non-Singaporean households is available. Consequently, the response addresses only resident household statistics and omits the requested non-resident data.

results.title: CPF Basic Retirement Sum Growth Projections beyond 2027 and Potential Five-Year Advance Planning Timeline
results.summary: The Ministry of Manpower will review the CPF Basic Retirement Sum (BRS) for members turning 55 after 2027, factoring in inflation and rising living standards. While specific growth projections beyond 2027 were not provided, the Minister confirmed that any changes will be announced with sufficient advance notice to enable planning. This approach mirrors the 2022 announcement covering cohorts from 2023 to 2027, which was released ahead of time. The Government has not yet committed to a fixed five-year advance planning timeline but will ensure members receive adequate notice for future adjustments.

results.title: Circumstances Leading to Discovery of Town Council Grant Disbursement Shortfall between FY2011 and FY2024
results.summary: The Housing and Development Board (HDB) identified a Service and Conservancy Charges Operating Grant shortfall between FY2011 and FY2024 during an internal review, facilitated by converting hardcopy reports to digital format for comprehensive checks. To prevent recurrence, HDB strengthened the grant computation process with additional reconciliation checks and verification steps. The Ministry of National Development advised Town Councils to consult their auditors regarding retrospective adjustments to financial and handover statements, noting that the materiality of underpayments varies across councils and requires professional judgement. This guidance ensures adjustments reflect specific circumstances while maintaining fiscal accuracy.

results.title: Clarification by Acting Minister for Culture, Community and Youth
results.summary: Acting Minister for Culture, Community and Youth David Neo issued a factual correction to his previous oral answer regarding the SPEx programme. He clarified that while athlete support increased by 450% from 272 in 1993 to over 1,500 today, the increase in sports covered is 300% (from 14 to 55 sports), correcting a prior statement of 350%. This update adjusts the official statistics for the number of sports supported since the programme's inception.

results.title: Classification Decisions for Oral Interviews with Mdm Kwa Geok Choo and Founding Leaders
results.summary: The Minister for Digital Development and Information clarified that National Archives of Singapore (NAS) oral history interviews are governed by individual agreements, with some founders like Mr CV Devan Nair and Mr S Rajaratnam permitting release five years post-death. While 80% of the collection is accessible, full release depends on NAS reviews for sensitivities regarding national security or private information, which may extend restrictions beyond agreed periods. The Albatross File excerpts for the Separation of Singapore from Malaysia have been released, with Dr Goh Keng Swee's interview partially redacted and Mr Lee Khoon Choy's fuller version pending. Mr Ong Pang Boon's interview is scheduled for release later this year following his 15-year withholding request, while other classified interviews remain restricted until sensitivities are resolved.

results.title: Complementarities between EatWise SG, Healthier SG and Age Well SG
results.summary: The Coordinating Minister for Social Policies and Minister for Health stated that the EatWise SG initiative's complementarity with Healthier SG and Age Well SG was addressed in the answer to Question No 43 during the Parliament Sitting on 4 November 2025. Details regarding public participation methods and the specific list of community care providers trained to recognise early malnutrition signs under this initiative were not provided in the current transcript. Members of the public are directed to refer to the previous parliamentary record for comprehensive information on these matters.

results.title: Criteria to Distinguish Scams from Civil or Commercial Disputes
results.summary: The Singapore Police Force distinguishes scams from civil disputes by assessing whether a seller had fraudulent intent to deceive victims into payments without providing goods or services. Cases involving impersonation of legitimate shops or sellers becoming uncontactable immediately after payment are classified as scams under the Penal Code. If a seller failed to deliver goods without fraudulent intent, no cheating offence is disclosed, and buyers must seek redress through civil remedies. Each case is evaluated based on its specific facts and circumstances rather than a fixed formula.

results.title: Cybersecurity Labelling Requirements of Consumer IP Cameras and Video-Recording Smart Doorbells for Local Sales and Usage
results.summary: The Cybersecurity Labelling Scheme (CLS) remains voluntary for consumer IP cameras and smart doorbells, though routers must meet Level 1 requirements. The Minister stated that the government is reviewing whether to mandate CLS assessment for additional devices like IP cameras due to evolving cyber threats, with any changes announced upon readiness. Consumers are advised to purchase CLS-labelled devices and adopt good cyber hygiene practices, such as updating software and avoiding default passwords, to mitigate attack risks.

results.title: Data on CPF LIFE Monthly Payout Recipients Aged 65 and above Paying Premiums in Cash Due to Insufficient MediSave Funds
results.summary: The Ministry of Health does not track the number of CPF LIFE recipients aged 65 and above paying annual premiums in cash due to insufficient MediSave funds. While most seniors are expected to have adequate MediSave balances, the Government provides support through premium subsidies for lower- to middle-income individuals, MediSave top-ups for Pioneer Generation seniors, and additional assistance for Merdeka Generation seniors. The Government assures that no Singaporean will lose MediShield Life or CareShield Life coverage due to financial difficulties.

results.title: Data on Family Justice Court Applications for Divorce Maintenance Compliance over Past Three Years
results.summary: Under the Women's Charter 1961 and Administration of Muslim Law Act 1966, the Family Justice Courts handle maintenance enforcement applications via iFAMS. Data from 2023 to 2025 shows combined application volumes for orders made during marriage and divorce, as a specific breakdown is unavailable. The Minister noted that enforcement applies when the obligated party fails to make required payments. These figures cover both wives, children, and incapacitated husbands without distinguishing between marital and divorce contexts.

results.title: Data on Government's Security Officers' Working Hours across Different Models and Proportion of Officers Working Overtime in 2025
results.summary: Since 2020, all Government Procuring Entities must adopt outcome-based contracts for security services unless strong reasons exist, with all entities having adopted at least one. The Government does not track specific data on the percentage of contracts using these models or the proportion of officers working 65+ overtime hours monthly in 2025. Welfare safeguards, including Police licensing conditions, remain in place to ensure fair working conditions. The Government will continue collaborating with tripartite partners to maintain reasonable working conditions for security officers.

results.title: Data on HDB Flats with Accrued Interest on Outstanding Resale Levies and Waiver Approvals for Second-time Subsidised Flat Buyers in Last 10 Years
results.summary: Under the resale levy policy effective since March 2006, second-time buyers pay the levy upon the later of the first flat's sale or the second flat's key collection, with no interest component. Previously, households selling between May 1997 and March 2006 faced a 5% annual interest on deferred payments. Since 2015, HDB has waived accrued interest for senior second-timers right-sizing to smaller flats and granted flexibility to others via appeal. Following the 2023 HDB Flat Eligibility letter implementation, HDB automatically waives accrued interest for all affected second-timer households without requiring an appeal.

results.title: Data on Post-natal Care Services and Costs, and Use of MediSave for Post-delivery Complications at Public and Private Hospitals
results.summary: The Government does not track average costs for episodic post-natal care services within eight weeks of delivery at public or private hospitals. While MediSave covers pre- and delivery expenses under the MediSave Maternity Package, it does not currently cover post-delivery complications; however, MediShield Life provides additional coverage for hospitalisation related to such complications. For mothers requiring outpatient services like lactation support or depression screening, financial assistance via MediFund is available through medical social workers at public hospitals for those facing affordability challenges. The Ministry of Health will continue reviewing financing schemes to ensure maternity services remain affordable while balancing MediSave adequacy with reasonable CPF contribution rates.

results.title: Data on Suspects and Proceeds Recovered from Gold Investment Scam Involving Genneva Pte Ltd
results.summary: The Police concluded their investigation into the Genneva Pte Ltd gold investment scam several years ago, with six individuals charged in 2019. Five were convicted between 2020 and 2025 of offences including fraudulent trading, cheating, and money laundering, while court proceedings continue for the remaining suspect. Authorities seized properties exceeding S$1.5 million and will apply to the Court for distribution to claimants. The distribution process is complex due to numerous potential claimants, and the Police will notify victims when they can file claims.

results.title: Data on Violations of Underage Alcohol Sales and Consumption at Licensed Liquor Sellers over Past Five Years and Plans to Enhance Enforcement Actions
results.summary: Between 2021 and 2025, licensed liquor sellers were recorded selling alcohol to or allowing underage consumption in 32, 23, 13, 11, and 12 cases respectively. The Police conduct regular enforcement spot checks at these premises, with frequency adjusted based on public feedback and operational observations. The Ministry has not announced specific plans to increase the frequency of these checks beyond the existing regular enforcement framework.

results.title: Detection of Toxic Air Pollutants Exceeding International Safety Limits in Jalan Kayu, Punggol or Seletar
results.summary: The National Environment Agency (NEA) confirms that ambient Volatile Organic Compound (VOC) readings, including benzene, at monitoring stations in Jalan Kayu, Punggol, and Seletar are well within international safety limits. Annual national benzene levels average approximately 0.3 parts per billion, significantly below the European Union's 1.6 ppb long-term exposure limit and US EPA acute exposure thresholds. Long-term data trends for key pollutants are published in the State of the Environment report, with the second edition scheduled for release later this year following the 2021 edition. In response to resident concerns, NEA will now provide ambient benzene data more regularly on its website, including specific geographical locations, to enhance public transparency.

results.title: Directing Graduates towards Sectors Maintaining Active Hiring and Wage Growth
results.summary: Most sectors maintained active hiring and wage growth in 2025, with Financial and Insurance Services, Professional Services, and Information and Communications showing strong demand for PMETs. These sectors held 14,200 vacancies as of September 2025, up from 12,600 the prior year, and recorded real median income increases exceeding the overall 4.3% growth. All Institutes of Higher Learning provide Education and Career Guidance services to direct students toward these opportunities. Additionally, new graduates can access career coaching from WSG and NTUC's Employment and Employability Institute, advisory from WSG's Volunteer Career Advisors, and explore pathways via the MyCareersFuture portal's CareersFinders feature.

results.title: Distinguishing Effects of School-based Learning Versus Private Tuition on Singapore Students' Performance in International Benchmarks
results.summary: The Minister for Education stated that it is not possible to conclusively partition educational outcomes into distinct parts attributable to school-based learning versus external tuition or enrichment. Research indicates that many inter-dependent factors, both within and outside schools, influence student learning. Consequently, the Ministry has not assessed the specific proportion of international benchmark performance attributed to each source or the methodologies used to distinguish their relative contributions.

results.title: Distribution of Performance Grades Given to Teachers Annually
results.summary: The Ministry of Education (MOE) applies a relative performance ranking system where teachers' annual grades reflect their standing against peers in the same substantive grade. Schools receive broad guidelines to assign C-, D, and E grades to the bottom 5% of the ranking pool, though deviations are permitted based on specific school contexts and teacher circumstances. A dedicated ranking panel assigns these grades after careful review, ensuring D grades are reserved for officers with poor relative performance rather than serving as a trade-off to meet C- thresholds. Most schools adhere to the guidelines, with only a few deviating upon strong justification.

results.title: Educating Parents on "Set, Think, Report, and Engage & Support" Framework in Context of Blind Box or Gacha Mechanisms Available Online
results.summary: The Minister confirmed that the "Set, Think, Report, and Engage & Support" framework, introduced by IMDA on 1 November 2025, applies broadly to online spending including blind boxes and gacha mechanisms. To support implementation, the Infocomm Media Development Authority launched resources on the Digital for Life portal tailored to parenting stages, while MDDI, MOE, and MSF jointly developed a Positive Use Guide offering practical tips on gaming limits. Specific outreach regarding loot boxes is conducted by the National Council on Problem Gambling to highlight gambling risks associated with incentivized purchases. These initiatives are reinforced through community workshops, MOE Cyber Wellness lessons, and social media campaigns targeting parents and youths to foster healthy digital habits.

results.title: Effect on Retail Tenant Mix and Leasing Terms at The Clementi Mall Following Acquisition by Owner of Neighbouring Mall
results.summary: The Ministry clarified that tenancy mix in commercial developments is market-driven, influenced by consumer demand and economic conditions rather than direct regulatory intervention. The Competition and Consumer Commission of Singapore (CCS) operates under a voluntary merger notification regime where parties self-assess competition concerns, though CCS retains the power to intervene if a merger substantially reduces competition or compromises consumer welfare. CCS will assess cases based on factual evidence, such as coordinated behavior to raise prices or reduce quality, rather than hearsay or projections. The public, including residents, can report potential anti-competitive behavior directly to CCS via hotline 1800 325 8282 or the FormSG portal at www.ccs.gov.sg. The parties involved in the Clementi Mall acquisition have not yet formally notified CCS for assessment.

results.title: Effectiveness of Existing MAS' Requirements on Banks to Protect Customers from Scams
results.summary: The Government and Monetary Authority of Singapore (MAS) have enhanced scam protection through Money Lock accounts, regular depositor reminders, and the progressive discontinuation of SMS One-Time Passwords (OTPs) for higher-risk activities like credit card provisioning. Banks are introducing additional safeguards this year, including cooling periods for high-risk transactions, in-app notifications for call verification, and stepped-up fraud surveillance requiring immediate consumer reporting of unauthorized credit card transactions. Data from the Mid-Year Scam and Cybercrime Brief 2025 indicates a 26% decrease in scam cases and a 13% reduction in losses in the first half of 2025 compared to 2024. Despite these improvements, MAS and financial institutions will continue reviewing measures against evolving scam tactics without complacency.

results.title: Emergency Communications between Parents and their Children given Smartphone Ban in Secondary Schools
results.summary: The Ministry of Education (MOE) confirms that existing school discipline policies regarding smartphone use during lessons remain applicable under tightened guidelines effective from 2026. For emergency communication, schools maintain protocols where parents can contact the general office to relay urgent messages, and students may use office phones to contact parents without owning personal handsets. MOE has provided implementation resources, including guidelines and FAQs, to support schools while noting that enforcement relies on individual school rules tailored to student needs. Feedback indicates broad parental support, though some schools continue to seek clarification on implementation details as they roll out the measures. The Ministry remains open to engaging with school leaders to address challenges and improve the overall school experience for teachers and students.

results.title: Enhancements and Follow-up Actions to Changi Point Area Following Charmingly Changi Competition in 2021
results.summary: Agencies are studying rejuvenation plans to transform Changi Point into a recreational node featuring new leisure, hospitality, and lifestyle experiences. Ideas from the 2021 Charmingly Changi competition are being incorporated where feasible, subject to the area's unique attributes. The Ministry has not provided specific timelines for implementation or details on long-term development strategies, stating that further information will be shared when plans are ready.

results.title: Enhancements to HDB Car Parks to Accommodate Electric Heavy Vehicles
results.summary: The Ministry of National Development confirmed that agencies are reviewing enhancements to HDB car park structures to accommodate electric commercial vehicles with an unladen weight exceeding 2,000 kilogrammes. This review, initiated in response to a Parliamentary Question in September 2025, addresses structural constraints caused by the heavy battery loads of these vehicles. The study regarding these structural enhancements is currently ongoing, with no specific timeline for completion or implementation provided at this stage.

results.title: Enhancing Recreational Fishing Access for Children and Families with Temporary Fishing Sites and Implementation of Rod Rental or Family Fishing Programmes
results.summary: The Minister for Sustainability and the Environment declined to open additional temporary fishing sites at ABC Waters @ Lorong 8 Toa Payoh during school holidays. She stated that heavy rain causes high-velocity water flow at the location, making it unsuitable for recreational use from a safety standpoint. Consequently, the proposal to pilot rod rental or family fishing programmes at this specific site was not approved due to these operational constraints.

results.title: Establishing One-stop Default Authorisation for Singaporeans to Allow Verification by Medical Institutions
results.summary: The Minister explained that Medical Claims Authorisation Forms remain mandatory to protect MediSave monies and data while preserving patient choice. Patients authorising all public institutions need to complete the form once, whereas those preferring specific institutions use a separate form. Institutions will prompt patients to sign during or after visits. The government has not established a default authorisation for Singaporeans to allow automatic verification and claims for inpatient and outpatient treatments at public hospitals.

results.title: Establishing Policy Framework for GST Relief on Rare or Complex Conditions' Essential Medications
results.summary: The Coordinating Minister for Social Policies and Minister for Health stated that the Government already absorbs Goods and Services Tax for medical services and medications for subsidised patients at Government-funded public hospitals, polyclinics, and intermediate and long-term care institutions. This existing arrangement applies to essential medications used to treat rare or complex conditions, thereby addressing high out-of-pocket costs for eligible patients. Consequently, the Ministry does not plan to establish a separate policy framework for GST relief on these specific medications, as the current absorption mechanism covers the intended scope.

results.title: Evaluation Criteria for Central Kitchen Pilot Project for Schools and Priorities behind Expansion to More Schools
results.summary: The Central Kitchen Meal Model (CKMM), piloted at Yusof Ishak Secondary School since 2022, was evaluated on operational efficiency, food quality, cost-effectiveness, and student satisfaction. The Ministry of Education expanded this model to 13 additional schools commencing January 2026 because the Individual Stallholder System remains the primary operating model, while CKMM serves as an alternative for schools struggling to recruit sufficient stallholders. This expansion aims to ensure students in these specific schools continue accessing healthy and affordable meals. The Individual Stallholder System will continue to be supported for schools not facing recruitment challenges.

results.title: Evaluation of Selective Door Operation Feasibility on North East Line to Boost Capacity
results.summary: The Land Transport Authority evaluated Selective Door Operation (SDO) for the North East Line and found it unfeasible. Implementing SDO would require longer dwell times for safe boarding, which would increase headways and journey times, thereby reducing system capacity rather than boosting it. Additionally, significant depot and track modifications would be needed to accommodate longer train sets. Consequently, this measure is not viable for enhancing capacity on the North East Line.

results.title: Exclusion of Regional Regulatory Authorities as HSA Reference Agencies and Impacts on Acceleration of Drug Access
results.summary: The Health Sciences Authority (HSA) currently recognises six reference agencies, including the US FDA and EMA, to facilitate an expedited 60 working days turnaround for therapeutic product registration. These jurisdictions are selected for their first-wave filing status, aligned scientific standards, and availability of English assessment reports. While HSA is actively exploring closer regulatory cooperation with other World Health Organisation Listed Authorities, including Japan's Pharmaceuticals and Medical Devices Agency and South Korea's Ministry of Food and Drug Safety, no specific technical barriers preventing their inclusion have been formally identified. Consequently, a feasibility study on recognising these additional authorities has not yet been conducted, though ongoing talks aim to accelerate drug access for regional populations.

results.title: Expanding ActiveSG Gym Facilities and Programmes in Woodlands Town
results.summary: Acting Minister for Culture, Community and Youth David Neo confirmed that Woodlands residents access ActiveSG facilities including the gym and Active Health Lab (AHL) at Woodlands Sport Centre. The recently renovated AHL extended operating hours to weekday evenings and Sundays to serve more residents. To improve senior accessibility, the Combat Age-Related Loss of Muscle (CALM) programme is offered at nearby Active Ageing Centres, specifically the NTUC Health AAC at Kampung Admiralty and the Sree Narayana Mission AAC at Woodlands. SportSG will explore partnerships with additional AACs, the People's Association, and Grassroots Organisations to bring more CALM runs to seniors in Woodlands in the future.

results.title: Expanding Financial Support and Training Infrastructure for Football Following Momentum from Singapore's Asian Cup Qualification
results.summary: In response to the national football team's historic qualification for the 2027 AFC Asian Cup, Sport Singapore (SportSG) is expanding financial and technical support for the Football Association of Singapore (FAS). Current measures include funding for local and overseas training, access to facilities at the Kallang Football Hub, and provision of football performance science and coaching expertise. For the upcoming Asian Cup, SportSG will specifically fund an overseas centralised training camp and deliver additional sports science support to build holistic capabilities. These actions aim to sustain momentum in football and other high-performing team sports through continued collaboration with National Sports Associations.

results.title: Expanding Support for SMEs to Develop Leading-edge Capabilities
results.summary: The Government maintains a suite of initiatives to support SMEs in adopting leading-edge capabilities like AI, digitalisation, and internationalisation. Specific grants include the Infocomm Media Development Authority's SMEs Go Digital programme, the Chief Technology Officer-as-a-Service, and the Digital Industry Singapore's Enterprise Compute Initiative for cloud credits and consultancy. The Partnerships for Capability Transformation grant facilitates collaboration with multinational corporations for supplier development and co-innovation. Additional support is available through the Enterprise Development Grant for innovation, the Productivity Solutions Grant for automation, and the Market Readiness Assistance Grant for overseas market promotion. Enterprise Singapore and sector agencies regularly review these solutions to ensure they meet evolving business needs, while SME Centres and the Singapore Business Federation provide advisory services and workshops for capability building.

results.title: Expected Singapore Population for Infrastructure Planning Parameter beyond 2030
results.summary: The Government does not plan infrastructure based on a single population figure but develops multiple scenarios accounting for demographic trends like birth rates, life expectancy, and migration. These scenarios indicate Singapore's total population is expected to remain significantly below 6.9 million by 2030. Consequently, the 6.9 million figure is retained as a relevant planning parameter for the 2030s to ensure preparedness for a range of possible outcomes rather than relying on one specific projection.

results.title: Extending CPF Matched Retirement Savings Scheme to Low-wage Workers below 55
results.summary: The Minister stated that the CPF Matched Retirement Savings Scheme (MRSS) maintains an age floor of 55 to target seniors nearing retirement rather than younger workers with longer savings runways. Consequently, the Government will not extend the scheme to low-wage workers below 55. Instead, eligible lower-wage workers in this age group can access the Workfare Income Supplement scheme for income and CPF savings support, while those in sectors under the Progressive Wage Model receive wage increases alongside skills upgrading.

results.title: Extending Silver Support Scheme Eligibility to Senior Residents in Executive Apartments with Low Per Capita Household Income
results.summary: The Minister for Manpower stated that the Silver Support Scheme remains targeted at seniors living in 5-room or smaller HDB flats, as it supports those with lower incomes during their working years and limited family resources. Seniors residing in Executive Apartments are currently excluded from this specific eligibility criteria. However, individuals in unique situations, including those with low per capita household incomes in Executive Apartments, may write to the Central Provident Fund Board for a case-by-case review of their eligibility. The Ministry will assess these circumstances holistically on an individual basis.

results.title: Extension of Guidelines for Designated Delivery Driver Parking Lots to Existing Malls
results.summary: The Urban Redevelopment Authority and Land Transport Authority issued a 2022 advisory note encouraging commercial malls to set aside delivery waiting bays for motorcycles and active mobility devices. This requirement applies to both new and existing developments, addressing the query regarding retroactive application to existing malls. Agencies are currently studying additional measures to make future developments friendlier to delivery riders, with further details to be shared when ready.

results.title: Fire Safety Standards for Mass Lithium-Ion Battery Storage in Workplaces and Measures to Mitigate Fire Risks
results.summary: The Fire Code mandates specific fire safety requirements for workplaces storing large quantities of lithium-ion batteries, including fire-rated compartments with energy limits, higher discharge density sprinklers, and mechanical ventilation systems to disperse flammable gases. These standards, aligned with international benchmarks, were established following extensive industry consultations and are regularly reviewed by the Singapore Civil Defence Force to ensure continued relevance. The Minister confirmed that existing measures address fire risks but did not announce new additional measures beyond the current code's stipulations.

results.title: Floor Area as Factor to Determine Occupancy Cap for Rental of Private Residential Properties
results.summary: The occupancy cap for private residential rentals is generally six unrelated persons to prevent neighbourhood disamenities. From 22 January 2024 to 31 December 2026, the Urban Redevelopment Authority temporarily raised this limit to eight persons for properties of at least 90 square metres. This relaxation addresses robust rental demand while maintaining residential amenities. The Minister stated there are no plans to further increase the cap, noting that larger floor area does not justify more tenants if it causes disamenities to nearby residents.

results.title: Further Enforcement Measures to Protect Vulnerable Bus Commuters from Fast-Moving Cyclists Near Bus Stops
results.summary: The Land Transport Authority (LTA) implements physical separation by building cycling paths behind bus stops where feasible. Enforcement actions target cyclists who speed or ride recklessly near stops, supported by "SLOW" markings and speed-regulating strips to remind riders to slow down. The Ministry assesses the sufficiency of these measures and enforcement in reducing near-misses, collisions, and perceived safety risks for vulnerable commuters, including seniors and persons with mobility needs, during boarding and alighting.

results.title: Government's Contributions to Pax Silica Initiative
results.summary: Singapore participated in the inaugural Pax Silica Summit with representatives from nine international partners, including Australia, Israel, Japan, and the US. The Ministry of Digital Development and Information's Permanent Secretary, Mr Chng Kai Fong, represented Singapore. Discussions focused on collaboration in advanced manufacturing and AI infrastructure, sectors identified as key industries for Singapore. The summit concluded with a non-binding Pax Silica Declaration committing participants to advance prosperity, tech progress, and economic security. Specific government contributions, involved local companies, and Key Performance Indicators were not detailed in the response.

results.title: Guidance for Installation of CCTVs at HDB Units to Ensure Neighbours' Privacy
results.summary: The Minister for National Development stated that while enforcement measures exist for CCTV installations facing neighbours' doors or windows, such disputes are rare. Residents are encouraged to resolve issues amicably first, seeking assistance from grassroots leaders or Management Corporation Strata Title if needed. As a last resort, parties may apply for mediation at the Community Mediation Centre or file claims before the Community Disputes Resolution Tribunals. The Minister did not specify distinct enforcement mechanisms or confirm if installation regulations are consistent across all HDB and private estates.

results.title: Impact of 2025 US National Security Strategy on Singapores Defence Interests and Strategic Positioning
results.summary: The Minister for Defence affirmed that Singapore's defence and security responsibility rests solely with Singaporeans, rejecting reliance on external rescue. Consequently, the nation maintains a strong Singapore Armed Forces (SAF) through mandatory National Service since 1967 and steady defence investments regardless of the security environment. While the US remains a key defence partner with strategic regional interests, the Minister emphasized that partnerships require real, substantive capabilities to add value. Singapore pursues defence diplomacy to foster mutual understanding and constructive bilateral and multilateral efforts for regional peace. The government adopts a long-term perspective on US relations despite potential annual shifts in the US National Security Strategy, ensuring the SAF builds unique capabilities for deterrence and operational purposes.

results.title: Implications of Increased Electricity Demands and Measures to Support Sustainable Growth and Expansion of Data Centre Capacity
results.summary: The Government assessed electricity availability before launching the December 2025 second Data Centre Call for Application (DC-CFA2) to ensure new capacity does not compromise other needs. Under DC-CFA2 requirements, at least half of new data centres must use green energy pathways aligned with the 2024 Green Data Centre Roadmap. The Minister stated this approach supports sustainable growth while maintaining resource balance. No specific timeline for accelerating renewable electricity development or import targets was provided in the response.

results.title: Implications on Responsibilities and Standing of Physicians with Graduate Diploma in Family Medicine Qualification
results.summary: In 2025, Family Medicine was officially recognised as a specialty, identifying senior physicians with advanced training beyond the Master of Medicine (Family Medicine) who manage complex chronic conditions in the community. These specialists lead multidisciplinary primary care teams, coordinate integrated care, and reduce unnecessary hospital referrals while continuing to partner with hospital specialists for cases requiring further management. Participation in corporate panels remains a commercial arrangement where all doctors must practice strictly within their accredited scope and clinical competencies. The Minister noted that physicians holding a Graduate Diploma in Family Medicine are strongly encouraged to pursue further postgraduate training, such as the MMed(FM) programme, to qualify for advanced training and register as Family Medicine Specialists.

results.title: Improving Accessibility for Seniors Commuting between Hougang Avenue 5 and Hougang Polyclinic by Adjusting Bus Routes
results.summary: The Acting Minister for Transport stated that LTA cannot adjust bus routes to add a stop directly at Hougang Polyclinic for seniors commuting from Hougang Avenue 5. This decision is due to the bus stop's proximity to the polyclinic's access road, which would impede safe vehicle movement and inconvenience pedestrians. Consequently, the current requirement for seniors to transfer buses or walk from a different stop remains in place, as amending routes at this location is not feasible under existing operational constraints.

results.title: Increasing Public Transport Vouchers for Lower-income Households to Defray Recent Fare Hike
results.summary: In 2024, Public Transport Vouchers for lower-income households increased to $60 from $50 to help defray fare hikes. The Government clarified that covering six months of fare increases is an indicative target rather than a strict cap. Households requiring additional support beyond the standard amount may submit appeals online or at local community centres and clubs. The Acting Minister for Transport confirmed these measures are available to assist eligible residents with recent fare adjustments.

results.title: Increasing Reservist In-camp Durations and Frequency in View of Uncertainties in Regional and Global Security
results.summary: The Ministry of Defence states that current In-Camp Training frequency, duration, and the statutory age limit for National Servicemen are broadly adequate for present security needs. While many reservists voluntarily extend service or contribute new skills, the SAF will conduct more intensive or specialised training in selected units requiring it. The Minister confirmed that any future general review of reservist commitments will be accompanied by a clear explanation of the rationale and will seek Parliamentarians' and public support in advance. No immediate plans to increase reservist commitments or raise the age limit were announced.

results.title: Indoor Heat Risk Assessment, Potential Subsidies and Cooling Solutions for Older HDB Public Rental Housing Blocks
results.summary: HDB is implementing cool coatings to mitigate heat across all estates, with application to existing public rental blocks scheduled during Repairs & Redecoration works by 2030. While heat-reflective coatings are prioritized, there are no plans to install or subsidize air-conditioning in public rental flats, as fans remain the preferred affordable cooling method for tenants. Lower-income households requiring assistance with rising heat costs may approach their Social Service Office for support. The board continues studying innovative solutions for all HDB estates, including public rental blocks.

results.title: Infrastructure, Financial Plans and Heat Stress Protection Policy Measures for Vulnerable Groups in Singapore
results.summary: The Government employs a multi-pronged heat resilience strategy comprising broad-based infrastructure upgrades and targeted interventions for vulnerable groups. Broad measures include launching the Heat Stress Advisory in 2023, activating islandwide cooling centres during heatwaves, and applying cool coatings to all HDB estates by 2030. Targeted support involves increasing greenery and fans in schools, installing hybrid cooling systems in new nursing homes and eldercare centres, and retrofitting roofs and walls with heat-reflective coatings and energy-efficient air-conditioning for vulnerable households. Lower-income families eligible for financial assistance may seek help from their Social Service Office. Agencies are currently studying the need for further adaptation measures, acknowledging that rising temperatures affect individuals differently based on health and living conditions.

results.title: Measures Addressing Involuntary Underemployment among Fresh Graduates and Junior Professionals
results.summary: The Ministry of Manpower (MOM) reports that involuntary non-time-based underemployment remains low and stable, comparable to other developed economies, while detailed trend findings are pending release. To assist fresh graduates and junior professionals, MOM has enhanced career matching services, launched the Singapore Opportunity Index, and deployed Workforce Singapore's Polaris for volunteer career advisors. Specific initiatives include Graduate Industry Traineeships to bridge experience gaps and employer support via the Productivity Solutions Grant and Career Conversion Programmes to facilitate job redesign. Concurrently, the Government is reviewing measures through the Economic Strategy Review to create roles aligned with resident workforce qualifications and skills.

results.title: Measures towards Industrial Air Pollution Reduction for North-Eastern Singapore and Cross-Border Air Pollution Public Reporting Framework
results.summary: The Minister for Sustainability and the Environment directed that the response regarding measurable targets for reducing industrial air pollution in north-eastern Singapore and commitments to publish periodic public reports on cross-border pollution incidents be found in the combined reply to Parliamentary Question No 69 at today's Sitting. No specific targets, timelines, or programme details were stated in this transcript. The matter was referred to the written record rather than being addressed verbally during the debate.

results.title: Monitoring Food Prices at Rental Coffee Shops
results.summary: HDB monitors food prices in rental coffeeshops via regular surveys but does not directly regulate prices. To support affordability, HDB recently enhanced funding for operators offering budget meals, requiring three specific options: an economy rice meal with one meat and two vegetable dishes, a halal meal, and a breakfast item. The Board ensures adequate supply across HDB towns to complement hawker centres. Beyond these guidelines, HDB does not impose nutritional requirements on budget meals or hawker stalls generally.

results.title: Monitoring Impact of Revised Integrated Shield Plan Rider Framework
results.summary: The Coordinating Minister for Social Policies and Minister for Health stated that details on monitoring the impact of the revised Integrated Shield Plan rider framework regarding private healthcare utilisation and public hospital waiting times were already provided in the answer to Questions No 1 to 4 during the Parliament Sitting on 12 January 2026. No new data, timelines, or specific operational constraints were introduced in this session. The Minister directed the inquiry to the previous parliamentary record for the requested information.

results.title: Monitoring of Effective Digital Usage Safety Initiatives for Children
results.summary: The Ministry of Digital Development and Information (MDDI), alongside MSF and MOE, is implementing a multi-agency approach to manage children's digital usage through four key family actions: setting online boundaries, thinking before acting, reporting inappropriate content, and engaging with children online. In November 2025, the Infocomm Media Development Authority released tailored resources on the Digital for Life (DfL) portal covering developmental stages and digital milestones. Concurrently, MDDI launched community-based digital parenting workshops, while the jointly developed Positive Use Guide on Technology and Social Media provides practical tips reinforced through MOE's Cyber Wellness lessons. The Ministry will continue monitoring effectiveness and strengthening initiatives via collaboration with parents, schools, and community partners.

results.title: Noise Dispute Resolution and Enforcement Measures in Residential Developments
results.summary: Frontline agencies, including the Housing and Development Board, initially resolve most neighbour noise disputes through engagement and mediation at Community Mediation Centres to preserve goodwill. For severe cases involving deliberate excessive noise where mediation fails, the Community Relations Unit (CRU) pilot triages and escalates matters to deploy noise sensors confirming the source and severity. Upon confirmation, the CRU Director-General can order nuisance-makers to cease and desist, a measure modelled on practices in the United Kingdom and Australia. The Ministry plans to expand the CRU pilot to more towns to strengthen enforceable protections for residents' night-time peace.

results.title: Noise Mitigation Measures for Residents near Tampines Expressway and Loyang Flyover Construction Work Sites
results.summary: Interim noise mitigation measures for the Tampines Expressway and Loyang Flyover include temporary barriers, machinery enclosures, restricted night works, and NEA-monitored noise meters. Post-completion, permanent barriers will be erected along the Loyang Viaduct facing residential blocks, while the new road section will utilize a noise-reducing pavement mix designed to absorb sound. The Ministry confirmed it will balance cost and technical feasibility to reduce noise impacts, noting that individual tolerance levels vary. If infrastructure-level measures prove inadequate, the Ministry will collaborate with other agencies to implement additional safeguards. A resurfacing schedule for existing expressways near residential areas is also in place, with the new construction already adopting the noise-reducing mix.

results.title: Number of Home Caregiving Grant Applicants and Top Conditions Cared for by Applicants
results.summary: Since the Home Caregiving Grant (HCG) launched in 2019, over 94,000 applicants have been recorded, with 74% aged 70 and above. The Ministry of Health does not track specific medical conditions for applicants. The HCG is means-tested and requires care recipients to be unable to perform Activities of Daily Living (ADLs) permanently, as certified by a doctor, rather than undergoing separate assessments. While concerns were raised regarding eligibility for caregivers of individuals with autism, intellectual disabilities, and mental health conditions, the Minister clarified that the existing ADL framework applies uniformly. The government noted that unclear cases could be surfaced to streamline the process, but no immediate changes to eligibility criteria or extension of the scheme were announced.

results.title: Number of Public EV Chargers Per 1,000 Battery Electric Vehicles
results.summary: There are approximately 220 public electric vehicle (EV) charging points per 1,000 registered EVs in Singapore. The Acting Minister for Transport stated that charger locations are generally evenly distributed across towns, with higher concentrations in Housing and Development Board areas exhibiting greater EV utilisation. No specific timeline for future deployment or additional eligibility constraints were mentioned in the response.

results.title: Nutritional Value Standards and Quality Checks for Budget Meals Initiative
results.summary: The Housing and Development Board (HDB) conducts regular price surveys for HDB coffee shop dishes but does not directly regulate food prices or impose minimum nutritional standards on budget meals. Recent enhancements to the initiative require operators to offer three specific meal options: an economy rice set with rice, one meat dish, and two vegetable dishes; a halal meal; and a breakfast item. While HDB provides funding support for these options, it deems it not feasible to mandate nutritional requirements beyond the existing guidelines, a stance consistent with regulations for general hawker stalls.

results.title: Participation Rate of Muslims in Wakaf Masyarakat Singapura and Plans to Enhance Outreach Efforts
results.summary: Launched in August 2024, the Wakaf Masyarakat Singapura (WMS) aims to create sustainable funding for the Singapore Muslim community. At least 150 Muslims have designated WMS as a beneficiary via wills, CPF nominations, or insurance policies. The Islamic Religious Council of Singapore conducts comprehensive outreach through partnerships with religious and corporate institutions to broaden contribution channels. Efforts include encouraging participation during key occasions like Ramadan, Haj, and the Islamic New Year to ensure all community segments understand and can contribute according to their means.

results.title: Patient Follow-Up Testing at Different Healthcare Cluster Polyclinics
results.summary: Patients may undergo follow-up tests, scans, or procedures at any polyclinic regardless of the public healthcare cluster of the ordering hospital. Test results are securely shared with the referring hospital via the National Electronic Health Record (NEHR) system. In some instances, a polyclinic doctor reviews the patient before tests are performed. Access to this data will become more seamless as Singapore transitions to a unified electronic medical record system across all public healthcare institutions.

results.title: Plans for Improving Employment Outcomes for Lower-income and ComLink+ Families
results.summary: The Ministry for Social and Family Development is reviewing strategies to enhance employment support for ComLink+ families facing barriers like caregiving and lower qualifications. Current measures include linking families with the Employment and Employability Institute (e2i) and social service agencies, while providing training support under SkillsFuture and Workfare. The ComLink+ Progress Package for Employment offers additional cash and CPF payouts to those earning better wages. Partnerships with e2i and TOUCH Community Services aim to build skills, manage work stressors, and facilitate job retention. The Ministry is also exploring financial incentives for jobseekers with lower education qualifications to upgrade their skills, emphasizing a whole-of-society effort involving community partners and employers.

results.title: Plans for Mandatory PMD Insurance to Protect Pedestrians and PMD Users
results.summary: The Acting Minister for Transport confirmed that third-party liability insurance is mandatory for commercial Personal Mobility Device (PMD) riders operating on public paths. For all other riders, the Ministry encourages but does not mandate the purchase of insurance to protect against claims. Consequently, there are no current plans to introduce compulsory insurance for non-commercial PMD users. The debate did not address specific timelines or eligibility criteria for future regulatory changes.

results.title: Plans to Review Safety Regulations for Transporting Workers in Rear of Lorries to Prevent Workplace Mishaps
results.summary: The Acting Minister for Transport confirmed that government agencies, alongside tripartite partners and NGOs, have implemented specific safety measures for workers transported in the rear of lorries. Current requirements mandate lorries be fitted with canopies and higher protective side railings, while also enforcing lower speed limits and minimum space requirements. The government stated it will continue engaging with industry and NGOs to explore further improvements to strengthen worker safety. No specific timeline for additional regulatory reviews was provided in the response.

results.title: Possibility of Legislation Similar to Australia's Social Media Ban for Those Under 16 for Singapore
results.summary: The Ministry of Digital Development and Information (MDDI) is actively assessing Australia's social media age ban, which took effect on 10 December 2025, while implementing its own progressive measures. Designated app stores must implement age assurance requirements by 31 March 2026, with plans to extend these to social media services to reduce exposure to age-inappropriate content. The Minister clarified that Singapore is not adopting a simple legislative ban but is pursuing a multi-dimensional approach addressing screen time, inappropriate content, and undesirable online behaviours under the existing Code of Practice for Online Safety. While acknowledging implementation challenges like bypassing measures, the MDDI continues consultations with stakeholders to co-create resources and programmes that foster healthy digital habits without relying solely on legislation.

results.title: Potential Adoption of United Kingdom's EV Charging Model to Speed Up Deployment in Singapore
results.summary: The Acting Minister for Transport stated that Singapore will not adopt the United Kingdom's model requiring users to bring their own EV charging cables for public chargers. This decision is based on public safety concerns, as fixed cables are currently mandated for most locations. Exceptions exist only in limited, restricted-access areas like private landed residences. The Minister noted that the option of user-provided cables will be reconsidered only as technology improves.

results.title: Preventing Spent Criminal Convictions for Child Abuse Offences
results.summary: The Minister for Social and Family Development confirmed that individuals convicted of relevant offences under the Children and Young Persons Act 1993 are disqualified from having their criminal records spent under the Registration of Criminals Act 1949. This measure applies to cases carrying stiff penalties of imprisonment and/or fine, ensuring robust screening for roles involving contact with children. Additionally, individuals convicted of egregious child abuse offences under the Penal Code, such as voluntarily causing grievous hurt or causing the death of a child aged below 14 years, are also disqualified from expunging their records as these fall within the Third Schedule of the RCA. The Ministry will continue collaborating with the Ministry of Home Affairs to enhance existing safeguards for child safety.

results.title: Proactive Identification of Singaporean Households under Public Rental Scheme in Potential Distress for Assistance
results.summary: The Housing and Development Board (HDB) actively monitors Public Rental Scheme households for financial distress indicators, such as ongoing rental payments without utilities. Upon identifying potential distress through rental data, feedback, or house visits, HDB collaborates with agencies like Family Service Centres and Social Services Offices to refer cases and provide support. The Minister confirmed that this proactive identification and referral process is an established operational practice for assisting Singapore Citizen households in need.

results.title: Proportion of Private Condominiums and Apartments Utilising BCA Accessibility Fund
results.summary: As of December 2025, the BCA Accessibility Fund has supported accessibility improvements in 161 buildings, including 20 private condominiums and apartments. Over the past five years, average annual utilisation was approximately $0.5 million. The Ministry is currently reviewing the fund to better support seniors in private residential developments, aligning with Age Well SG initiatives. No dedicated new fund has been announced yet, though enhancements are under consideration.

results.title: Proposal For Highest Tier of Silver Support for Seniors Above 80 Who Live in Smaller HDB Flats
results.summary: The Silver Support Scheme provides tiered payments to seniors aged 65 and above based on lifetime wages, housing type, and household support, ensuring those in smaller flats with fewer resources receive higher amounts. While the Minister confirmed that eligibility criteria are periodically reviewed, he did not commit to merging flat types to equalize payments immediately, citing the need to balance fiscal prudence with sustainability over the medium to long term. Additional support measures like the Workfare Income Supplement and Majulah Package bonuses are available to boost retirement savings for lower- to middle-income working seniors. Seniors requiring further assistance for basic living needs can apply for ComCare through their nearest Social Service Office.

results.title: Proposal to Publish Real-time Train Arrival Data on LTA DataMall
results.summary: The Land Transport Authority will not publish real-time train arrival data on the LTA DataMall. The Acting Minister for Transport stated that short intervals between train arrivals and adequate on-station communication to commuters render such data unnecessary. Consequently, no implementation timeline or technical impediments were discussed, as the measure is not being pursued.

results.title: Raising Awareness of Cooling Spaces under Mercury Taskforce Heatwave Response Plan and Prioritising Access for Vulnerable Communities
results.summary: Under the Mercury Taskforce heatwave response plan, air-conditioned cooling centres will open islandwide at community facilities near residential areas upon heatwave activation. Official channels will disseminate locations and operating hours, while capacity management ensures priority access for vulnerable groups including seniors, low-income households, rental dwellers, and outdoor workers. The Government encourages community cooperation to maintain capacity limits and facilitate access for those most at risk. No specific timeline for plan activation or detailed publicity campaign schedule was provided in the response.

results.title: Raising Awareness on Benefits of Strength Training for Seniors
results.summary: The Health Promotion Board and Sport Singapore have launched initiatives to raise awareness of strength training, including issuing physical activity guidelines, conducting outreach, and offering structured programmes like Start2Move and the Healthy 365 app. ActiveSG gyms provide onsite trainers and introductory programmes to assist less active adults in their 40s and 50s who lack confidence with gym equipment. While functional outcomes are tracked for some structured programmes, detailed data is not currently available and would require a further Parliamentary Question. Regarding the request for a new ActiveSG facility in Admiralty, Woodlands, the Ministry will consider the need but will not pre-empt the written answer. The Healthy 365 app is highlighted as a comprehensive starting point for residents seeking guidance on healthy living.

results.title: Reasons for 55% Increase in People's Association's Expenditure for Year Ended March 2025
results.summary: The People's Association's expenditure for activities and projects rose 55% to $1,136 million in FY2024 compared to $733 million in FY2023. This increase was primarily driven by the Community Development Council (CDC) Vouchers Scheme, which expanded from two to three tranches to provide direct financial relief to households. The additional tranche facilitated payments to heartland merchants, hawkers, and supermarkets. Furthermore, higher spending supported a greater volume of events and programmes organised by grassroots organisations, CDCs, and the PA for residents across Singapore.

results.title: Reasons for Installation of New Traffic Violation Enforcement Cameras at 11 Locations and Future Deployment Plans to Other High-risk Areas
results.summary: The Traffic Police installs mobile traffic violation enforcement cameras at 11 locations selected based on higher risks of accidents and violations. Future deployment to other high-risk areas, such as school zones, will depend on prevailing risk assessments. The Minister stated that cameras may be redeployed or additional units deployed as needed. No specific timeline or eligibility criteria for new locations were provided in the response.

results.title: Reasons for Sharp Decline in Dengue Cases Reported in 2025
results.summary: In 2025, Singapore recorded 4,036 dengue cases, the lowest since 2018, driven by Project Wolbachia, global case declines, and absence of local outbreaks. Project Wolbachia reduced transmission risk by over 70% in release areas but is not a standalone solution; it will expand to 740,000 households by March 2026 and 800,000 (50% of all households) by end-2026. Expansion pace depends on finite resources and local mosquito suppression effectiveness rather than a single bottleneck. NEA will announce new release sites based on historical dengue data and current *Aedes aegypti* populations, with requests for specific areas like Nanyang noted for future consideration.

results.title: Recidivism Rates for Child Sexual Abuse Offences over Past 10 Years
results.summary: Minister K Shanmugam presented Table 1 showing two-year and five-year recidivism rates for local offenders incarcerated for child sexual abuse offences where the re-offence was also child sexual abuse. He noted that these rates are generally low but subject to fluctuation due to the small annual number of such offenders released. The Minister did not provide specific 10-year recidivism rates or data for the full past decade as requested.

results.title: Reports of Foreigners Using their Vehicles for Business and Trade Purposes in Singapore
results.summary: The Land Transport Authority has received reports of foreign-registered vehicles being used for commercial purposes in Singapore, though the validity of the drivers' work passes remains unconfirmed. The Ministry will collaborate with other Government agencies to study measures preventing foreigners without valid work passes from using their vehicles for business or trade. No specific timeline or immediate enforcement action has been announced at this stage.

results.title: National Council of Social Service (Amendment) Bill
results.summary: The National Council of Social Service (Amendment) Bill reclassifies NCSS as a Group 1A public body and expands its mandate to develop the wider social service ecosystem, incorporating feedback from approximately 300 member organisations. Under the new framework, NCSS shifts from a specific-stakeholder focus to a sector developer role with a streamlined single-tier membership structure and no fees, while the Council size expands to between 15 and 27 fully appointed members, with at least half nominated by sector representatives. The Bill addresses concerns regarding governance, compliance burdens, and innovation by establishing a systemic platform to coordinate with existing agencies like MSF and AIC, ensuring clarity on roles and data-informed planning. While supporters commend the extensive consultation and substantive changes, MPs raised points on proportionate standards for smaller agencies, appeal processes for administrative decisions, and the need to balance government priorities with professional independence and frontline representation.

results.title: Review Feasibility of Monthly Payouts for Silver Support Scheme and Reduce Differentiation by HDB Flat Size
results.summary: The Minister for Manpower declined to review the Silver Support Scheme for monthly payouts, noting that quarterly payments provide seniors with greater flexibility to manage larger expenses over three-month periods. Switching to monthly payments would delay receipt of funds for the second and third months of the quarter. Additionally, the Minister maintained that differentiating payments by HDB flat size ensures higher support for seniors in smaller flats who are more likely to have fewer financial resources. No changes to the current payment frequency or differentiation criteria will be made.

results.title: Review of Lifetime CPF Contribution Threshold for Silver Support Scheme
results.summary: The Ministry periodically reviews Silver Support Scheme eligibility, having doubled the lifetime CPF contribution threshold from $70,000 to $140,000 in 2021 and raising the monthly per capita household income limit from $1,800 to $2,300 in 2025. Concurrently, quarterly payouts increased by 20%, benefiting approximately 290,000 seniors aged 65 and above. While Members questioned the rigidity of the $140,000 threshold relative to the 37% rise in the CPF Full Retirement Sum, the Minister emphasized a holistic approach that integrates other support packages like the Assurance Package and GST Vouchers. The Government maintains that balancing fiscal sustainability with targeted uplifts across the seniors' entire working life journey ensures adequate support without altering the current eligibility framework.

results.title: Review on Feasibility of Online versus In-person Cultural Orientation Programme for S Pass Holders
results.summary: The Cultural Orientation Programme (COP) for S Pass holders launched in December 2023 as a voluntary online initiative to familiarize participants with Singapore's norms and regulations. As of December 2025, 6,574 holders have completed the programme, with approximately 95% reporting the content was useful. The Ministry of Manpower selected an online format to maximize reach, noting that additional community activities organized by groups like the People's Association remain available. The review concluded that the online approach effectively achieved programme objectives, though the transcript did not specify a final decision on adopting a more community-based in-person approach.

results.title: Reviewing Animal and Veterinary Service Protocols during Trapping Operations Following Elderly Dog Incident in Seletar
results.summary: The Minister for National Development stated that existing Animal and Veterinary Service protocols for assessing senior dogs' health, mobility, and stress during trapping operations were already in place. He confirmed these protocols were not reviewed following the recent Seletar incident involving an elderly dog's death, noting the matter was previously addressed in a Ministry reply on 12 January 2026. No new measures or changes to the operational procedures were announced during this session.

results.title: Reviewing Income Ceiling for Executive Condominiums
results.summary: The Minister for National Development confirmed that the Ministry of National Development and HDB are reviewing the monthly household income ceilings for Executive Condominiums ($16,000) and subsidised flats ($14,000) to ensure affordability for middle-income households. This review follows concerns regarding rising EC prices and a small $2,000 differential between flat types. An update on the outcome of this review will be provided when ready. The Government remains committed to maintaining public housing accessibility for Singaporeans.

results.title: Reviewing Per Capita Household Income Benchmark for ComCare Applicants in View of Increased Cost of Living
results.summary: The Minister for Social and Family Development confirmed that the ComCare per capita household income benchmark is reviewed every two to three years. The most recent update occurred in July 2023. No immediate review was announced in response to recent cost-of-living increases, as the existing schedule remains in effect. The current benchmark continues to apply to means testing for ComCare applicants until the next scheduled revision.

results.title: Reviewing Temasek's Risk-return Framework to Value Strategic Ecosystem Building alongside Commercial Returns
results.summary: The Minister for Finance directed that the question regarding a review of Temasek's risk-return framework to value strategic ecosystem building alongside commercial returns has already been addressed. The response was provided in the reply to Parliamentary Questions Nos 12 to 14 on the Order Paper dated 12 January 2026. No new measures, timelines, or alternative capital support mechanisms were announced during this session. Further details are available in the referenced parliamentary record.

results.title: Reviews and Enhancements to Sports Excellence Awards to Support Elite Athletes' Training, Welfare and Long-term Development
results.summary: The Acting Minister for Culture, Community and Youth outlined a holistic High Performance Sport system supporting over 1,500 carded athletes across 55 sports through bespoke Sport Excellence (spex) programmes, including the 2024 spexPotential initiative for emerging talent and inaugural spexEducation Undergraduate Scholarships. While the Major Games Award Programme (MAP) remains a private scheme managed by the Singapore National Olympic Council (SNOC), the Ministry will request SNOC to consider recognising SEA Games Silver and Bronze medallists during their recent review, with updated awards expected in due course. Funding effectiveness is assessed holistically via medal outcomes, performance metrics, and athlete development rather than solely on regional tournament results, with feedback gathered through daily interactions, the Athletes Commission, and NSA partnerships.

results.title: Road Conversion Status and Progress for Footpaths, Cycling Paths or Bus Lanes
results.summary: Of the 60 road repurposing sites identified in March 2021, six projects have been completed and five are currently in progress. The Land Transport Authority is conducting stakeholder engagement and detailed engineering studies for each site before implementation. Additionally, some sites have been converted under the Friendly Streets initiative, a programme scheduled for expansion to all towns by 2030. The transcript does not specify the reasons for delays at individual sites beyond the ongoing studies.

results.title: Security Implications for Singapore and Region Given Policy Shifts in 2025 US National Security Strategy Paper
results.summary: The Minister for Defence directed the Member to refer to the written reply to Parliamentary Question No 8, asked by Mr Yip Hon Weng, for the Sitting on 13 January 2026, regarding security implications of the 2025 US National Security Strategy paper. No further details on specific security impacts or changes to US defence cooperation were provided in this oral response. The substantive answer to the questions on regional security and the prioritisation of the Western Hemisphere remains pending in the referenced written document.
